name: The Fresh One Speaks
description: Kell has spent four years cross-referencing Elder notation against Hollow behavioral patterns and believes the fresh Hollow in the lair communicates through a body language descended from the Elder People's gesture notation. He wants to know what it is trying to say.
accept_text: Kell opens a second notebook — newer than the main one, recently filled. 'Four years of documentation. The ancient ones move very rarely, but when they do, the movements are precise. Grammatical.' He walks you through the key sequences: approach-and-acknowledgment, the listening-angle, the attention-indicator. 'If it has something to say, these will open the channel. The reply may be slow. Give it time.' He demonstrates the listening posture. 'The key is this one — the specific angle that in Elder notation means I am listening and I have understood what you showed me before. If it responds to that, it's been waiting for someone who knew the language.' He hands you the second notebook. 'Bring it back with whatever you observed — every motion, every sequence, in order. I can translate anything you don't understand in the moment.' He pauses. 'It marked the wall for a reason. It waited near the entrance for a reason. Something about the Lazarus modification connects to it directly, and I think it knows that too.'
complete_text: You use the sequences Kell taught you. For a long moment nothing happens. Then the Hollow Vassal moves — not the twitching of something animal, but something with grammar. It orients north, then south, then north again. It holds. It draws in the air: the Elder symbol for 'continuing' or 'unfinished.' It holds. Then the wall symbol — the method, the process. Then its palms turn upward in the gesture Kell called plea or warning. It repeats the full sequence once, exactly. Then it goes still. You watch its face during the silence that follows. Not blank — present. The ancient ones behind it do not move. On the long walk back to the maintenance tunnels, the translation assembles in the way Kell said it would. You report every motion, every pause, every hold. Kell translates. Three concepts: Not finished. Still building. Stop them. He's quiet for a long time after. 'It has been watching the lair since before the war,' he says finally. 'It saw the first version of this. It's watching the new version. And it knew, eventually, someone would come who could almost understand it.' He closes the notebook. 'We need to talk to Sergeant Kehl.'
